title: Charred Herbal Thai Fish Satay
description: Charred, shaggy, cozy Thai (Isaan) dish featuring brown sugar, deglaze, pescatarian-friendly. Perfect for slate-tile cooking.

ingredients.name: 500g firm white fish (such as cod or tilapia)

instructions: Preheat a slate-tile grill to medium-high heat, about 200°C (400°F).
Thread 500g of firm white fish onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
Brush the fish with 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill the fish for 2-3 minutes per side, or until charred and cooked through.

Meanwhile, mix together 2 tablespoons of brown sugar, 2 tablespoons of fish sauce, 2 cloves of minced garlic, 1 tablespoon of grated fresh ginger, and 1 tablespoon of Thai red curry paste in a small bowl.
Once the fish is cooked, brush the glaze all over the fish while it's still on the grill.
Transfer the fish to a plate and garnish with 20g of fresh Thai basil leaves, 40g of fresh cilantro leaves, 20g of chopped peanuts, and 2 tablespoons of chopped fresh mint leaves.
Serve immediately, with additional lime wedges and honey on the side if desired.
Let the fish rest for 5 minutes before slicing into thin strips and serving.
